commands.review_summary:
The PR adds stable event identity fields and redacted-body markers to diagnostics-otel log exports, carries diagnostic-only log semantics through logger/subsystem paths, and updates tests and OpenTelemetry docs.

PR surface: Source +283, Tests +194, Docs +9. Total +486 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows withheld diagnostic logs still export `body: "log"` and lack stable event identity fields; the PR body also lists focused local and Testbox validation for the new shape.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review should decide whether to accept the exported telemetry contract change; no narrow automated repair is indicated.

target.title: improve: add OTEL event identity for redacted logs
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Resolves a problem where operators could keep OTEL diagnostic log bodies private, but then had too little stable structure to query exported logs safely. Ordinary redacted `log.record` diagnostics needed low-cardinality event identity from the logging source path, and trusted `security.event` records needed the same OTEL event identity without being routed through message-body redaction semantics.

## Why This Change Was Made

Diagnostic log export now keeps message bodies private by default while adding stable semantic fields: `eventName`, `otel.event.name`, `openclaw.signal.type`, and `openclaw.log.*` event/category/outcome/reason attributes. The source logger now carries diagnostic-only semantics from subsystem logs without adding those fields to ordinary console or file output; trusted security records stay on their existing structured `openclaw.security.*` schema and now add `openclaw.security.<action>` event identity. Generic diagnostic attributes are blocked from spoofing reserved semantic keys, and reserved fields are assigned before generic attributes.

## User Impact

Operators can query every exported OpenClaw OTEL log by stable event names and safe structured attributes without enabling `diagnostics.otel.captureContent: true`. Security analytics also get explicit `security.event` identity while preserving the existing security field shape.

## Evidence

Behavioral proof:

- Focused diagnostics-otel tests cover redacted diagnostic `log.record` export, trusted `security.event` export, stdout JSONL event identity, spoofed reserved keys, and attribute-count pressure around reserved semantic fields.
- Logger tests cover diagnostic semantics derived from logger metadata, forged reserved/internal marker rejection, and source subsystem logs carrying explicit diagnostic semantics without changing ordinary console/file output.
- Documentation now describes stable diagnostic log identity fields, redacted bodies, stdout `eventName`, and separate trusted security-event export identity.

Verification:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic-log-events.test.ts src/logging/subsystem.test.ts` passed: 28 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts` passed: 95 tests.
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md` passed.
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Testbox through Crabbox passed on `blacksmith-testbox` lease `tbx_01kwdz4cjn225amtx5qfj4n5q3` with `corepack pnpm check:changed --base HEAD --head HEAD -- docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts src/logging/diagnostic-log-events.test.ts src/logging/logger.ts`.
target.author: jesse-merhi
